title: epipactis paludosa
intro: Epipactis paludosa, also known as the marsh helleborine, is a species of orchid found in Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may be beneficial in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Epipactis paludosa are said to help alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as reduce inflammation in the digestive system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
